nodes.label: Work Proposal: Human Approval Gate Rule
nodes.norm_label: work proposal: human approval gate rule

nodes.label: Work Item Lifecycle (proposed -> completed)
nodes.rationale: Work files use a sortable YYYYMMDD-NNN naming convention so execution order matches file sort order. Completed proposals remain in work/1_proposed/ with status=completed and a link to the completion report, keeping history visible without duplication.

nodes.label: Template Retention Rule
nodes.rationale: Templates are durable reference artifacts; deleting or renaming them breaks discoverability for future agents. Superseding a template requires adding a new version rather than removing the prior one.

nodes.label: Fail-Closed Runtime Configuration Default
nodes.rationale: Production configuration must fail safely rather than fall back to insecure defaults. Debug or insecure fallbacks require explicit opt-in (e.g. DJANGO_DEBUG=true) to preserve local/CI ergonomics without compromising production security.

nodes.label: WCAG 2.2 Level AA Accessibility Standard
nodes.rationale: Minimum accessibility standard for all user-facing interfaces. Requires keyboard navigation, focus management, accessible names/alt text, non-color-only state communication, and programmatic association of form fields with labels.

nodes.label: Test Coverage Baselines (94% backend, 89%/80% frontend)
nodes.rationale: Backend must maintain 94% overall coverage; frontend must maintain 89% statement and 80% branch coverage. New code paths need at least one test; untested branches must be justified in the work item.

nodes.label: Convention Domain Model (core fields)
nodes.rationale: Required fields: start date, end date, free-text location, organizer-provided timezone (IANA identifier via selectable UI), one continuous daily open-hours window, and maximum attendance capacity.

nodes.label: IANA Timezone Selection via Selectable UI
nodes.norm_label: iana timezone selection via selectable ui

nodes.label: Curated Timezone List (one city per UTC offset)
nodes.rationale: Reduced from 500+ zoneinfo.available_timezones() to 30 curated entries (one major city per UTC offset) to improve UX without breaking IANA identifier compliance. Addresses root cause (list length) rather than symptom (cumbersome native select).
nodes.norm_label: curated timezone list (one city per utc offset)

nodes.label: Django ModelForm Server-Authoritative Validation
nodes.rationale: Replaced custom field parsing with Django ModelForm to reduce hand-rolled parsing logic, keep error shape stable, and improve code idiomaticity.

nodes.label: ICE Gate Bypass by Agent (ponytail mode override — process violation)
nodes.rationale: Agent implemented before creating proposal because ponytail mode prioritized shipping over process. Resulted in a retroactive proposal artifact. Documents that ponytail mode does not override the ICE gate.
nodes.norm_label: ice gate bypass by agent (ponytail mode override — process violation)

nodes.label: Proposal-to-Completion Traceability (Status:completed + link)
nodes.rationale: Proposals remain in work/1_proposed/ with Status:completed and a link to the completion report, rather than being moved or deleted. Keeps completed work visible from the proposal file.

nodes.label: Expectation Boundary Separation (criteria in 3_expectations/, not intent)
nodes.rationale: Measurable success and acceptance criteria were moved from intent and architecture docs into active expectation files under 3_expectations/ to enforce the Expectations > Context > Intent priority ordering.
nodes.norm_label: expectation boundary separation (criteria in 3_expectations/, not intent)

nodes.label: Pattern Context Scope Guidance (area-specific pattern files vs architecture)
nodes.rationale: Architecture context and pattern context are distinct categories; scoped pattern files are recommended when guidance is area-specific rather than folding it into 2_context/architecture.md.
nodes.norm_label: pattern context scope guidance (area-specific pattern files vs architecture)

links.source_location: The gate is a mandatory enforcement step in the ICE methodology
Discoveries section — agent failure explicitly documents the gate violation
Approval Gate section — retroactive artifact due to agent error
Metadata section — links back to proposal
Both concepts govern how work artifacts are organized and discoverable
Intent Delta — curated list is a UX scope reduction of the IANA selection requirement
